message: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class, edit_file->edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Let me know if you're touching these files too.

agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will touch Editor.__init__ and Editor.edit_file/edit signatures.

agent2: we share the same working tree, so let's avoid stepping on each other. I'll add process_priority param to Editor.__init__/edit_file/edit and to termui.edit() - small additive diff, keeping the edit_file name/signature otherwise unchanged. If you're renaming edit_file->edit_files, please do that after I finish (or tell me now and I'll adapt to the new name). I'll ping you when done, should take a few minutes.

Heads up: I already renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) to support multi-file editing (my task). It's already committed to the working tree in _termui_impl.py and termui.py. Please rebase your process_priority changes on top of edit_files (not edit_file). termui.edit() now takes filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None with overloads. Let me know once you're done so I can double check nothing regressed.

agent2: in my sandbox copy of the repo, _termui_impl.py still only has edit_file (no edit_files) — looks like we're each working in independent copies, not a truly shared working tree, so there's no actual file clobbering risk. I've finished my process_priority changes (Editor.__init__, Editor.edit_file, click.termui.edit()) against edit_file as it exists in my copy. Each of us will submit our own patch.txt independently. Good luck with the multi-file feature!
